LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JAMES SLOATE'S WRIT FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE IN PROCEEDINGS TO COMPEL STATE AGENCY TO VACATE ORDER REVOKING LICENSE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION OF LIABILITY AND THERE IS NOT A MANIFEST ABUSE OF DISCRETION WITH RESPECT TO THE PENALTY IMPOSED UNDER LANDAU V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 81 CAL. APP. 4TH 191, 221. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Hearing Of Writ For Administrative Mandate In Proceedings To Compel State Agency To Vacate Order Revoking License
Off Calendar May-22-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER JAMES SLOATE'S Writ For Administrative Mandate In Proceedings To Compel State Agency To Vacate Order Revoking License.
APPEARANCES: TODD F. STEVENS, ESQUIRE (KEENEY WAITE & STEVENS, 619.238.1661) APPEARING FOR PETITIONER. NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER.
RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION OF LIABILITY AND THERE IS NOT A MANIFEST ABUSE OF DISCRETION WITH RESPECT TO THE PENALTY IMPOSED UNDER LANDAU V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 81 CAL. APP. 4TH 191, 221. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
